architecture.summary: The paper uses sparse autoencoders (SAEs) trained on autoregressive transformer language model activations to decompose hidden states into interpretable features. These features are assembled into causal circuits using attribution patching with integrated gradients (AtP-IG) to identify which features causally influence the model's preference for garden-path vs. non-garden-path sentence continuations. Information flows from embeddings through residual stream and attention/MLP features to output logits, with causal relevance determined by indirect effect on the probability difference between competing readings.
trained_components: sparse autoencoders (SAEs) on residual stream and attention/MLP outputs
structural probe MLPs for dependency relation prediction

biblio.title: Incremental Sentence Processing Mechanisms in Autoregressive Transformer Language Models

claims.statement: While many important features LMs use to process garden path sentences relate to syntactic structure, some reflect syntactically irrelevant heuristics
Many of the most important features LMs use are interpretable and syntax-related; however, some uninterpretable or spurious features exist

claims.dataset: adapted Arehalli et al. (2022) garden path dataset (72 sentences, 3 structures: NP/Z, NP/S, MV/RR)
claims.statement: LMs' representations encode multiple interpretations of ambiguous garden path sentences simultaneously, with both pro-GP and anti-GP features having non-zero average activations
Causal interventions on subject and object feature detectors reverse the model's garden-path reading preference, while random feature interventions do not
LMs do not re-use features from garden path sentence processing to answer follow-up questions; feature overlap (IoU) between initial reading and GPRC circuits is 0% for NP/S and 0.2% for NP/Z
LMs do not repair their initial incorrect representations via reanalysis when answering follow-up questions; they generate new features that are not syntactic
Circuit faithfulness values of 0.20 (NP/S) and 3.48 (NP/Z) indicate that the identified features do not capture the complete mechanism; many important features remain unidentified

claims.dataset: adapted Arehalli et al. (2022) garden path dataset
claims.statement: Pythia-70m shows garden-path biases in NP/Z and MV/RR sentence structures but prefers non-garden-path continuations for NP/S sentences
